decisions.decision: Treat this step as a review-only boundary check, not a cleanup pass: keep the existing helper-level additions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` as acceptable support work unless they are found to widen into route wiring, legacy payload rewrites, or reset-specific behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Prune the helper-level tests simply because this step is scoped to helper assembly
Re-open earlier implementation work and broaden this step into a general cleanup pass

decisions.decision: Keep the scope confined to shared health assembly in `backend/server.py`: do not touch `route_request()`, `service_index_payload()`, frontend health clients, or checked-in docs, so `/v1/health` remains a private helper-only surface until the later routing step.
alternatives_rejected: Advertise or reference `/v1/health` before the router serves it
Interpret 'no router wiring yet' narrowly and allow other public-surface references to move ahead early

decisions.decision: Treat `health_payload()` as frozen legacy behavior for this step and preserve `/health` plus `/healthz` exactly as they are; `v1_health_payload()` should remain an additive copy-and-extend helper rather than refactoring the legacy payload builder.
alternatives_rejected: Refactor `health_payload()` internally even if the emitted JSON stays identical
Collapse legacy and v1 health responses onto a new shared base helper in this step

decisions.decision: Preserve the current in-process, probe-free helper assembly: keep uptime sourced from `PROCESS_START_MONOTONIC`, leave `reset_state()` untouched, avoid new reset-specific assertions, and do not add live `db`, `inference`, or `auth` dependency probes in this boundary check.
alternatives_rejected: Add test-only reset hooks or negative reset coverage as part of this step
Introduce runtime dependency probes now because the status vocabulary already supports `degraded` and `unhealthy`

steps.task: Inspect the health-helper block in `backend/server.py` and confirm the shared helpers stay additive: `v1_health_payload()` copies `health_payload()`, component records come from the existing shared builders, and top-level status plus uptime are assembled from in-process state only.

Sanity-check the surrounding boundary for scope creep by verifying that `route_request()`, `service_index_payload()`, frontend health consumers, docs, and `reset_state()` remain unchanged, and that the existing helper tests stay limited to helper composition rather than route wiring or reset behavior.
If any out-of-scope edits are present, trim them back to the helper boundary without changing the legacy `/health` or `/healthz` payload contract; otherwise leave the implementation intact.
Review the resulting diff against the parent plan to confirm this step remains a shared-health-assembly slice only, then commit the step artifacts once the boundary check is complete.
